exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you go down to the kitchen to get me some water?
After school, the kids usually go down to the basketball court.
She doesn't like taking the elevator so she usually goes down by the stairs.
exampleSentences: Anh có thể xuống nhà bếp lấy giúp em ít nước không?
Sau khi tan học, các em thường xuống sân chơi bóng rổ.
Chị ấy không thích đi thang máy nên thường xuống bằng cầu thang bộ.

definitions.vi: Di chuyển từ vị trí cao xuống vị trí thấp.
definitions.es: Moverse de una posición más alta a una más baja.
definitions.en: To move from a higher to a lower position.

example.en: I am going down the stairs.

notes:
xuống = to go down, move downward. It’s the opposite of lên (go up).

Use it for physical movement from a higher place to a lower one: stairs, floors, hills, vehicles, or going from upstairs to downstairs. It also works for direction in general: getting off, coming down, stepping down.

Very common in movement verbs: đi xuống, chạy xuống, bước xuống, leo xuống.  

It can also mean something decreases: prices, mood, quality, energy (giảm xuống, “go down” in level).
notes_md:
**xuống** = to go down, move downward. It’s the opposite of **lên** (go up).

Use it for physical movement from a higher place to a lower one: stairs, floors, hills, vehicles, or going from upstairs to downstairs. It also works for direction in general: getting off, coming down, stepping down.

Very common in movement verbs: **đi xuống, chạy xuống, bước xuống, leo xuống**.  

It can also mean something decreases: prices, mood, quality, energy (**giảm xuống**, “go down” in level).
